name: VT with Pulse - Stable (Monomorphic)
identifyBy: Regular wide complex tachycardia - rate 100–250 bpm
AV dissociation: P waves dissociated from QRS (if visible)
Fusion beats or capture beats confirm ventricular origin
management: Confirm haemodynamic stability - BP, mental state, any instability → immediate cardioversion
Amiodarone 150mg IV over 10 minutes, then 1mg/min infusion (preferred)
Investigate and correct reversible causes: electrolytes, ischaemia
All VT requires cardiology follow-up - ICD evaluation
pearls: All wide complex tachycardia should be treated as VT until proven otherwise
Haemodynamic stability does NOT mean the rhythm is SVT - stable VT is common
Brugada criteria, Vereckei algorithm: systematic tools to confirm ventricular origin
Lidocaine: second-line; less effective than amiodarone for sustained VT
After successful cardioversion: oral antiarrhythmic bridge to EP study/ICD
differentials: SVT with aberrant conduction (narrow becomes wide)
